mappings.rationale: Directly addresses the Balfour Declaration, Jewish settlement, partition of Palestine, establishment of Israel, Arab-Israeli wars, the intifada, and peace processes in B.1.c and B.3.c.
Directly addresses Iran since 1925 including the Pahlavi regimes, the 1978-79 Revolution, the Islamic Republic, and the Iran-Iraq war in C.1.
The Iran-Iraq War, invasion of Kuwait, and the Persian Gulf War are directly discussed in B.3.d and C.1.c.
Directly addresses African colonial rule, nationalist movements, independence, and post-independence developments throughout D and E.
Political developments in sub-Saharan Africa -- one-party states, military coups, and democratic transitions -- are central to E.4-8.
The African National Congress, the antiapartheid movement, and the establishment of black majority rule in South Africa are central to E.8.b.i.
Islam's role across the region -- from the Arab states to the Islamic Republic of Iran and North African politics -- pervades this section.
Arab nationalism and the Ottoman Empire legacy shaped the political structures of Southwest Asia in outline B.
Islamic law is relevant to Iran's Islamic Republic and the introduction of Islamic law in Sudan in C.1.c and E.5.b.
The rise of Islamic fundamentalism in Egypt, Tunisia, Algeria, and Iran connects to B.3.e, C.1.c, and D.2.
The Balfour Declaration, Jewish settlement in Palestine, the Biltmore Resolution, and the establishment of Israel are central to B.1.c.
Jewish identity and culture are relevant to the establishment of Israel and immigration to Palestine in B.1.c and B.3.c.
Central to the section, tracing the end of European colonial rule and emergence of new nations across Southwest Asia and Africa.
British mandate territories (Palestine, Iraq, Transjordan), British colonies in Africa, and dissolution of British colonial rule are all discussed here.
European colonial empires in Africa and Southwest Asia and their dissolution form a central narrative of this section.
Postcolonial nation-building challenges, ethnic tensions, and the legacy of colonialism shaped newly independent states throughout.
African nationalist parties, Arab nationalism, Kurdish separatism, and independence movements shaped the region throughout.
The Lebanese civil war, Angolan and Mozambican civil wars, Somali clan warfare, and Sudanese civil war are all addressed.
The Arab-Israeli wars, the Iran-Iraq War, the Persian Gulf wars, and the Algerian War of Independence are central to this section.
Terrorism in the region -- including PLO activities, the intifada, and Libya's disruptive role -- is addressed throughout.
Apartheid, political persecution, ethnic strife, and genocide in Rwanda and Burundi highlight human rights abuses across the region.
Apartheid in South Africa, racial discrimination in Rhodesia, and racial dimensions of colonialism are central to E.8.
Palestinian refugees, Somali refugees, and displacement from civil wars across Africa are discussed throughout.
Ethiopian socialism under Mengistu and Soviet involvement in African conflicts are relevant to outline E.
Struggles for democratic governance in Africa -- from military coups and one-party states to multiparty elections -- connect to outline E.
Authoritarian regimes -- Ataturk, Nasser, Qaddafi, Mobutu, apartheid South Africa, and military governments -- pervade this section.
The Egyptian revolution (1952), the Iranian revolution (1978-79), and the Algerian War of Independence reshaped the region.
Fascist Italian domination of Libya (1922-42) and the Italian conquest of Ethiopia (1936) are discussed in D.1.b and E.5.a.
Superpower involvement in African conflicts and Cold War dynamics in Southwest Asia shaped the post-independence era.
UN peacekeeping in the Congo, intervention in Somalia, sanctions against Libya and South Africa, and the mandate system are all discussed.
The mandate system, Camp David Accords, peace talks, and international negotiations are discussed throughout.
The mandate system, UN trusteeship, international sanctions, and legal frameworks governing decolonization are relevant throughout.
The post-WWI mandate system, division of former German colonies, and dissolution of the Ottoman Empire connect to B.1, D.1, and E.1.
WWII's impact -- North African campaigns, Allied occupation of Iran, and accelerated decolonization -- shaped the entire region.
Ba'thist ideologies in Syria and Iraq, Ethiopian socialism, and ujamaa socialism in Tanzania are discussed across outline B and E.
Ataturk's secularization program in Turkey and the tension between secular and religious governance connect to A.2.
The AIDS pandemic in southern Africa is mentioned in E.8.b.iii for Botswana, Lesotho, and Swaziland.
The geopolitical significance of oil in the Arabian Peninsula and Cold War competition in Africa shaped the region's trajectory.
Economic development challenges and aid programs for newly independent states across Africa and Southwest Asia are discussed throughout.
Oil-based economies, economic development challenges, and the transition from colonial to independent economic systems connect to the section.
Economic transformation -- including oil discovery in the Arabian Peninsula and postcolonial development challenges -- is relevant throughout.
The settlement of borders in the mandate system, partition of Palestine, and territorial disputes across Africa and Southwest Asia are central themes.
The Jewish diaspora and return to Israel and the Palestinian diaspora connect to B.1.c and B.3.c.
Separatist church movements and religious dimensions of African societies under colonial rule and after independence are discussed in E.8.a.iii.
Arab-Israeli wars, the Iran-Iraq War, and counterinsurgency campaigns are discussed throughout.
Poverty and development challenges facing newly independent African nations connect to E.4-8.
